Class HolySpirit

java.lang.Object
DivinePerson
HolySpirit
public class HolySpirit extends DivinePerson
HolySpirit: third Person of the Trinity, fully God and distinct Person.

This class centers on pneumatological identity and relation-of-origin description. The Holy Spirit is modeled as consubstantial with Father and Son, personally distinct, and confessed as "Lord and giver of life" in the creed.

References: - Full divinity: Acts 5:3-4; 2 Cor 3:17. - Distinct personhood: John 14:26; John 16:13-15; Matt 28:19. - Creedal confession: Nicene-Constantinopolitan Creed. - Catechism: CCC 243-248.

    Constructs the Holy Spirit Person with full possession of the shared divine nature.

  • Constructor Details

    • HolySpirit

      public HolySpirit(DivineNature divineNature)
      Constructs the Holy Spirit Person with full possession of the shared divine nature.

      Using the shared DivineNature instance maintains the model's monotheistic structure while preserving personal distinction by relation.

      Parameters:
      divineNature - the one divine nature possessed fully by the Holy Spirit

  • Method Details

    • relationOfOriginDescription

      public String relationOfOriginDescription()
      Provides the Holy Spirit's relation-of-origin descriptor in this model.

      The returned phrase follows Western Catholic formula: procession from the Father and the Son "as from one principle." The wording is chosen for doctrinal precision in this project's teaching scope.

      Specified by:
      relationOfOriginDescription in class DivinePerson
      Returns:
      the Holy Spirit relation-of-origin phrase
